element_introduction_order.element: Eight original fighters (Ryu, Ken, Blanka, Guile, Chun-Li, Zangief, Dhalsim, E. Honda)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic fighting mechanics and command inputs through early matches against these characters

element_introduction_order.element: Four Grand Masters (Balrog, Vega, Sagat, M. Bison)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Single-player arcade mode - final bracket opponents
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Encountered as increasingly difficult CPU opponents in late-game progression; later made playable for competitive depth
Visual presentation that reinforces character identity; provides spatial context for match

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Single-player mode - final additional match opponent
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Advanced competitive feature allowing mastery verification against identical character clone with alternate palette
Cosmetic variation system used to distinguish players in mirror matches

typical_relief_triggers: Staff roll sequence with new background music
typical_tension_triggers: Grand Master encounters in arcade ladder
Mirror match challenge against identical opponent
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Round-based tension with variable match length; tension escalates through arcade ladder progression with Grand Masters as climactic encounters; relief provided by ending sequences and character-specific victory cinematics

difficulty_curve.early_game: Eight original fighters presented with balanced fighting styles; Ryu and Ken differentiated for player choice variety
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased opponent skill and mechanics as player progresses through arcade ladder matchups
difficulty_curve.late_game: Four Grand Masters as final opponents; considerably toned down from CPU-only World Warrior versions but remain relatively strong; M. Bison serves as final boss encounter
notable_spikes: Transition from original eight fighters to Grand Masters bracket
M. Bison final match - end-of-ladder climax

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear sequence of 12 total fighter matchups (8 original + 4 Grand Masters + 1 mirror match); each opponent represents increasing difficulty; culminates in Grand Master final four and character-specific ending
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Single-player arcade mode: Ryu → Ken → Blanka → Guile → Chun-Li → Zangief → Dhalsim → E. Honda → Balrog → Vega → Sagat → M. Bison

notable_level_patterns.description: Each fighter has distinct stage background with character-specific color palette; visual design reinforces fighter identity and provides thematic coherence
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Character-specific stages (Ryu's Japan dojo, Blanka's Brazil jungle, etc.)

notable_level_patterns.description: Final arcade mode opponent is a cloned version of player's selected character with alternate color palette; serves as mastery verification and equilibrium test
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mirror match vs. player-selected character with blue name tag and alternate palette
